prompt: Construct a Meridian Academic Publishing & Fulfillment Portal supporting offline-first account access for students, instructors, academic staff, and administrators who manage academic content submissions, reviews, and physical issue fulfillment from a single web experience. The Dioxus-based UI presents a role-aware dashboard after local username/password login, with clear navigation to User Center, Orders, Submissions, and After-Sales. Users can edit profiles, manage US shipping addresses with a single default address, set invoice titles for printable receipts, and control notification preferences limited to in-app inbox banners. Students and instructors create multi-round submissions using guided templates, can resubmit up to 10 times before the stated deadline, and always see version history with timestamps in MM/DD/YYYY and 12-hour time; downloads apply visible watermarks. Order & fulfillment screens support subscription periods (monthly, quarterly, annual), line-item quantities, split/merge when an order contains multiple series, and a reconciliation view. Reviews and after-sales flows let users post an initial review plus one follow-up within 14 days, attach up to 6 images (5 MB each), and track return/refund/exchange cases through statuses from Submitted to Closed, with SLA timers. The backend uses Rocket to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by the Dioxus frontend. MySQL persists all data with RBAC enforcing access and an auditable trail of permission changes. Authentication is local-only with salted password hashing and 30-minute idle session expiry. Content metadata governance runs on save with sensitive-word detection. File handling enforces format allowlists (PDF/DOCX/PNG/JPG), size limits (25 MB per file), and file signature verification. Payments are implemented as a pluggable offline module recording Cash/Check/On-Account transactions with idempotent posting rules, escrow-style holds, refunds, and nightly reconciliation reports.
